channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Starfield’s Terran Armada Achievements Uncover Secret Features
item.content:encoded:
Terran Armada drops in just two days, bringing Starfield fans everywhere the long-awaited second major expansion for Bethesda Game Studios’ space-faring epic. It has been more than a year since the last expansion, Shattered Space, broadened the game’s horizons, but until just a couple of weeks ago, Bethesda was standing silent on the next expansion.

We know now it’s called Terran Armada, and we also know what the point of the expansion is. And, thanks to recent achievement lists being posted online, we know the DLC’s hidden features that are yet to be revealed.

Don’t read on any further if you don’t want the core elements of Terran Armada uncovered.

Starfield Achievements Reveal Unseen Elements

There are a whopping twenty achievements (and now, of course, PlayStation Trophies) inbound to Starfield for Terran Armada and the Free Lanes update, which drops in just a couple of days on April 7.

These achievements, which are easily viewable on Steam at your own risk, detail some important features of Starfield that we’ve not yet been privy to. Bethesda Game Studios might have revealed stacks of information about the next update, but not everything was explained.

For instance, there’s ‘This for That’, an achievement that requires players to build and hand over an outpost to a faction, which is an all-new feature. There’s also a triple pack achievement of sorts related to the new ‘Incursion’ mechanic in Terran Armada. This will see players engage in massive space battles, and there’s an achievement for completing five, then fifteen, then thirty Incursions.

Delta, the new robotic companion introduced with Terran Armada, has a couple of achievements. There’s one for fully upgrading them, and another to ‘convince Delta that you are worthy of the title Captain’.

What has fans most excited is the arrival of a new city (or a super-sized ship of sorts) in Terran Armada. There’s an achievement called ‘Master of Magnetism’ that requires players to use a magnet to destroy a robot in New Babylon.

Here’s the full list of achievements coming with Terran Armada and Free Lanes on April 7:

  • Lost Luxury – Complete Lost Luxury
  • Into the VOID – Complete Into the VOID
  • Battle of the Unifier – Complete Battle of the Unifier
  • This for That – Build out and hand over an outpost to a faction
  • Greater Than – Convince Delta that you are worthy of the title Captain
  • Less Than – Prove the superiority of artificial life
  • Incursion Fighter – Clear out 5 Incursions
  • Incursion Exterminator – Clear out 15 Incursions
  • Incursion Eliminator – Clear out 30 Incursions
  • Overclocked – Fully upgrade Delta
  • Half the Battle – Let Sarah Morgan know about her action figure
  • Fully Equipped – Find a spaceship equipment module schematic and install an equipment module on your ship
  • Top Tier – Apply a Rank 4 Legendary Effect to a piece of your equipment
  • Just Like Grandma Used to Make – Eat the questionable food offered by a spacefaring old woman
  • They’re Not Toys! – Collect all action figures
  • Contract Killer – Kill 10 targets for the Arbitrator
  • Stay of Execution – Stop Bernhard from killing Caruso
  • Master of Magnetism – Use a magnet to destroy the robot for Ivica in New Babylon
  • Riddle Management – Prevent any deaths by answering riddles
  • Quantum Hoarder – Retrieve your inventory from another universe

Are you eager to unlock the full list of achievements in Starfield? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders has more fixes inbound

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Shigeru Miyamoto Wants Pikmin Included in Everything Nintendo Does
item.content:encoded:
Shigeru Miyamoto has created so many legendary Nintendo properties. Donkey Kong, Mario, Star Fox, and The Legend of Zelda were all created by Miyamoto. In the 2000s, he also created Pikmin. Now, he says he’s on a mission to include Pikmin in any product he can.

Right now, Miyamoto is totally focused on The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, which recently hit theaters. Prior to the film’s release, it was revealed that Pikmin would appear in the movie in some capacity. Along with Star Fox, it seems that the legendary producer is trying to call his own shot and feature as many of these legendary characters on the big screen as possible.

Shigeru Miyamoto’s Pikmin Mission

During an interview with IGN, Miyamoto was asked whether he requested that Pikmin be featured in the film. In response, he says it was much more serious than a request. He’s personally on a mission to feature Pikmin everywhere.

“Now I wouldn’t say a request,” he said. “More like a mission. I’m on a mission to try to include Pikmin in any kind of Nintendo product we put up. Obviously Pikmin can be appearing anywhere, but the idea, the setting of Galaxy really lends itself to having Pikmin be there as part of the world. So I was waiting in anticipation for what kind of ideas came from illumination.”

The Pikmin were also featured in a Nintendo animated short, “Close to You, in 2025. It was the first animated release from Nintendo’s in-house studio. The Pikmin may also be coming to the Nintendo Switch 2 with an enhanced release of Pikmin 4. Learn more about that here.

How do you feel about Miyamoto’s passion for Pikmin? What would you like to see from a Pikmin movie or fifth game? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: McDonald’s Launches Gamer Menu With Anti-AFK Tool in Türkiye
item.content:encoded:
McDonald’s in Türkiye has launched a new menu centered around gamers, and the unique hook is a tool that is reminiscent of the brand’s iconic golden arches that players hook around their controllers to prevent them from drifting away from the action.

Unfortunately, for gamers and burger lovers across the globe, the menu and device are only available in Türkiye right now. The “Pro Gamer Menu” itself features a Big Mac, medium fries, a medium Coke, and an 8-piece order of onion rings.

Stay Away from AFK with McDonald’s’ Archie

This new menu emphasizes the importance of ensuring that taking a break for food doesn’t result in being kicked out of the game or interrupting your or other players’ progress. The solution is “Archie,” a tool that was designed by the TBWA\Istanbul marketing agency.

The item, which looks like the McDonald’s logo, wraps around the analog sticks of any controller with side-by-side thumbsticks and prevents you from being booted for being away.

“Being away from the screen during online gameplay—especially in high-intensity moments—can directly disrupt the flow of the game. That is why many players turn to different workarounds. We transformed this behavior into a gamer insight and made Archie part of the McDonald’s experience by offering it as a gift to customers who order the Pro Gamer Menu,” the press release for this menu notes.

It’s definitely a fun collector’s item, and if it works out, perhaps they will sell more or sell them globally. For now, McDonald’s Türkiye is simply looking for how to make an impact with creative campaigns.

What do you think about the menu and the Archie device? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1029)—Answers for Sunday, April 5
item.content:encoded:
It’s a sleepy Sunday, but before you spend the day relaxing, you’d best complete today’s NYT Connections puzzle. It’s not so tough, but I’ve got the best answers and hints you need below to get it wrapped up as quickly as possible, so it’s one less thing for you to worry about.

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections hints and answers you need for today’s challenge.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 5)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • PIPE
  • PANCAKE
  • PULPIT
  • LIGHT SWITCH
  • SHELL
  • VIOLIN
  • MUSHROOM
  • ORBIT
  • COIN
  • PASTEURIZE
  • NUCLEUS
  • MAGNIFYING GLASS
  • ELECTRON
  • GOOGOL
  • DEERSTALKER
  • THE BIRD

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – These words are used to describe parts of an atom
  • Green – These words can be associated with a classic investigator, often seen with his dear ‘Watson’
  • Blue – These words represent things you can flip
  • Purple – These words start with a term for a squishy substance

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 5:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 5 (#1029)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – ELECTRON, NUCLEUS, ORBIT, SHELL
  • Green – DEERSTALKER, MAGNIFYING GLASS, PIPE, VIOLIN
  • Blue – COIN, LIGHT SWITCH, PANCAKE, THE BIRD
  • Purple – GOOGOL, MUSHROOM, PASTEURIZE, PULPIT

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-april-5-1029/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ARC Raiders Crafting Changes Are ‘Just The Start’ of Pain Point Updates
item.content:encoded:
In the recent Flashpoint update in ARC Raiders, developer Embark Studios added a new map condition, two new ARC constructs, some fresh weapons, quests, and a bunch of quality-of-life improvements and bug fixes. One such change was around the in-game crafting system, which has been made more intuitive and streamlined following mass player feedback over the last few months.

To cut down on the time spent crafting, Embark Studios has made the process seamless, auto-filling required components from players’ collections and reducing the amount of navigation needed to make stuff ahead of raids.

This keeps the flow of the game moving quickly, but Embark Studios has advised it’s just the beginning of a much more conscious effort to streamline the game.

Embark Studios has always touted a community-first approach to developing ARC Raiders, the exciting extraction adventure game that dropped at the end of October. The title became an overnight sensation, but it wasn’t long before players started finding faults with the game’s base formula.

Unfortunately, some of those issues are only just being addressed by Embark Studios, including the cumbersome approach to crafting items in-game.

In a recent blog post, the team explained that all-new changes to the game’s crafting systems were made in a bid to appease the community:

A lot of you pointed out that gathering missing materials for a craft was more work than it should be. You already knew where to get what you needed, maybe from recycling something you had lying around, or picking it up from Celeste, but jumping through multiple screens to actually do it was a pain point. That friction was getting in the way of the fun.

To resolve that pain point, a change was made:

We’ve introduced a streamlined way to acquire missing materials directly from the crafting screen. Now, when you’re missing something for a craft, you’ll see a new Acquire Resources button. Tap it, and you’ll get a clear list of every available source where you can get what you need, whether that’s recycling, buying from Celeste, or something else, so you can fill the gap and complete your craft without losing your flow.

The keyword here is available: if a material is obtainable through your current options, you’ll see it, and you can act on it immediately. No more hunting through menus. No more abandoning crafts out of frustration.

But at the base of that blog post, more changes were teased. The team explained that this ‘doesn’t fix everything’, and that more changes are coming to address the ‘pain points’ that have been plaguing players for the longest time. It was promised that the team would keep ‘chipping away’ at these issues, producing more alterations soon.

What do you want to see changed in ARC Raiders? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Gray Zone Warfare continues to fly on Steam

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: Mass Effect TV Show Could Be Written To ‘Appeal to Non-Gamers’
item.content:encoded:
Mass Effect has been up in the air for several years. The launch of Mass Effect: Andromeda did little to instil confidence in the community, and the years-long wait we’re experiencing for whatever is coming next is not enjoyable. The fans sitting there, hungry, are also eagerly awaiting something to emerge from the TV show that has been talked about for more than a decade.

Well, in a recent report from The Ankler, that ‘something’ surfaced. The Head of Global TV at Amazon MGM, Peter Friedlander, offered up a tidbit about the Mass Effect TV show, but it’s not all good news.

Commander Shepard Isn’t in the Mass Effect TV Show

The concept of the live-action adaptation is one that has swollen in popularity in recent years, making for some stellar, award-winning projects. The likes of The Last of Us and Fallout have broken records and enjoyed monumental moments in the spotlight, and both are yet to deliver everything they have to offer.

However, both approached expertly tackled something that crops up when making a live-action adaptation of a video game. They were delivered in a way that makes them perfectly digestible by non-gamers unfamiliar with the source content.

The Last of Us, for instance, had an opening segment that perfectly explained the origin and potential of the Cordyceps annihilation that wiped out most of humanity. This wasn’t in the game, which dropped in 2013.

Now, Amazon MGM’s Head of Global TV, Peter Friedlander, has gone on record talking to The Ankler about the Mass Effect TV show, which has been floating around in one form or another for more than a decade.

In the interview, Friedlander explained he’s soaking up scripts and trying to determine the future of Mass Effect on the Prime platform, but it’s looking like it, too, will be written in a way that holds non-gamers in mind. This will ensure it remains as accessible as possible, which is important when you’re dealing with a universe as grand and multi-faceted as Mass Effect.

Thanks to IGN for spotting the interview.

There’s a lot of history and lore there.

Last year, Mass Effect’s spearhead, Mike Gamble, explained that the television show will not be a rehash of the original story, nor would it feature Commander Shepard. Instead, it’ll be an original story set after the trilogy that started in 2007.

What do you want from the Mass Effect TV show? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about Crimson Desert’s latest patch

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.
